summary: Refactored the entire JS↔C# communication layer into a unified message protocol. Backend: created MessageDispatcher.cs — single entry point for all entity actions (group.*, tag.*, profile.*, proxy.*) using a typed envelope: JS→C# sends { type: 'request', id, action, payload }, C#→JS replies { type: 'response', id, success, error, data }. Frontend: rewrote bridge.js as DuckBridge with request/response/push semantics and backward-compat alias _duckBridge; rewrote Profiles.js to wire all Group/Tag CRUD (list, create, edit, delete) via DuckBridge.call() with live dropdown refresh; rewrote CreateProfileModal.js to load Groups/Tags from backend on open and support quick-create from dropdown actions.
current_status: Backend: MessageDispatcher.cs compiles, all entity handlers (group, tag, profile, proxy) registered in one dispatcher. Frontend: bridge.js unified protocol active, Profiles.js CRUD wired to backend, CreateProfileModal.js Groups/Tags loaded from backend. App builds with 0 errors. Phase 3-5 marked complete in plan.
